title: betula alba var. mandshurica
intro: Betula alba var. mandshurica, also known as the Manchurian white birch, is a deciduous tree species belonging to the Betulaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Betula alba var. mandshurica include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and soothe respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of tea, tincture, or salves, often used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and digestive issues.
